_doc: Calibration set for the L2 ABSTAIN gate (kleos-lib/tests/abstain_calibration.rs). Each query is labelled `answerable` (a relevant memory exists in the store) or `unanswerable` (none does). `sem_top`/`ce_top` are the best semantic_score (raw cosine) and best ce_confidence (normalized cross-encoder) a real retrieval produced for that query. The harness sweeps the semantic threshold through the production `evaluate_signals` logic and reports false-abstain rate (FAR, answerable wrongly abstained) and true-abstain rate (TAR, unanswerable correctly abstained), asserting FAR<5% / TAR>80% at the shipped default (0.65). PROVENANCE: the scores below are seeded from the agent-in-the-loop evaluation distribution (answerable top cosine ~0.71-0.78, unanswerable ~0.45-0.62, with deliberately hard boundary cases). They are a regression baseline and a template -- before freezing a production threshold for a NEW embedder/reranker, RE-CAPTURE per-query sem_top/ce_top from an isolated kleos-server (real bge-m3 + reranker, scratch DB) running each `q` against the `corpus`, then update these numbers and re-run the sweep. `corpus` exists for that live re-capture; the offline evaluator uses only class/question_type/sem_top/ce_top.

corpus.content: Calyx stores all primary state in PostgreSQL with a Redis cache layer
The Borealis project is led by engineering lead Marin Okafor
The Cobalt service migrated to its v2 storage backend on 2026-02-14
We chose Rust for the engine because of memory safety and predictable latency under load
Vexel uses SQLite with the sqlite-vec extension for its embedded vector store
My preferred editor is Helix with the catppuccin theme
Kleos listens on port 4200 by default
We run the BAAI/bge-m3 embedding model at 1024 dimensions
For German retrieval the HTTP reranker with bge-reranker-v2-m3 outperforms the English Granite cross-encoder
Dahlia is paused pending a security review of its ingest path
